title: Achillea subcartilaginea
intro: Achillea subcartilaginea, also known as Mongolian yarrow, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments, including wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Achillea subcartilaginea include flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Achillea subcartilaginea is commonly used in traditional medicine and is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, although the effectiveness and safety of its use have not been extensively scientifically studied.
